preamble: The nasalized low vowel [ɑ̃] shifts toward the near-open front nasalized vowel [æ̃] through a process of fronting and raising. This occurs in contemporary French dialects as part of ongoing nasal vowel chain shifts, driven by articulatory pressure and acoustic perceptual factors. In nasal contexts, vowels tend to undergo raising due to the aerodynamic effects of nasal airflow.

examples.context: Ongoing contemporary chain shift; /ɑ̃/ raising and backing part of counterclockwise shift
Prenasal vowel raising in European Portuguese
Vowels raised in nasal contexts; nasal vowels became lower in Modern French period
